description: E2E cross-language parity tests for schema-driven Date/DateTime matching. Each language loads the column schema, runs each query against the same row set, and must produce identical matched-id lists.

tests.name: datetime_iso_literal_gt
tests.description: Basic DateTime column > ISO literal (Z offset).

tests.name: datetime_moscow_naive_string_with_col_tz
tests.description: Naive ISO strings interpreted in column.tz='Europe/Moscow' (UTC+3).

tests.name: datetime_ny_dst_fall_back_earlier
tests.description: Decision 19: fall-back '2026-11-01 01:30' NY resolves to EDT (05:30 UTC), earlier of the two occurrences. Narrow the window to late-2026 so only the fall-back value qualifies — if the 'later' (EST, 06:30 UTC) instance were picked the row would not match the < 06:00 UTC bound.

tests.name: datetime_ny_dst_spring_forward_skipped
tests.description: Decision 19: '2026-03-08 02:30' NY is in the spring-forward gap — matcher returns null/false, row skipped.

tests.name: datetime_ny_after_fall_back_unambiguous
tests.description: 02:00 NY on 2026-11-01 is unambiguously EST (07:00 UTC) — past the fall-back transition.

tests.name: datetime_epoch_seconds_with_unit
tests.description: Decision 21 + column.unit='s': int treated as epoch seconds.

tests.name: datetime_in_list
tests.description: IN-list with ISO literals; row 5 sub-ms truncates to 21:00:00.000 and matches third item.

tests.name: datetime_microsecond_truncation_to_ms
tests.description: Decision 23: sub-ms precision is truncated. Row 5's .000500Z compares equal to .000Z.

tests.name: datetime_unparseable_record_skipped
tests.description: Row 4's garbage values fail to coerce; record skipped (not matched).
